title: Teaching
text:
I am committed to accessible and relevant education in quantitative methods for social science research. Here you will find all the materials I have created and/or used during my teaching experience.

Teaching Philosophy

⬇ Download my teaching philosophy
My teaching philosophy is grounded in the idea that learning is a cumulative and reflective process, where students build on prior knowledge to develop complex skills and concepts. As an instructor in quantitative methodology, I focus on bridging students’ existing knowledge with new material through individualized support, interactive review sessions, and open communication. My goal is to foster student autonomy by making statistical reasoning accessible, practical, and meaningful to each student’s own research interests and background.

“We teach […] what we do; we do […] what we teach.” — George Cobb (2014)

title: Home
text:
Bladimir Padilla
    Graduate Student · PSQF · University of Iowa

    Hi,

    
      I am a graduate student in Educational Measurement and Statistics, broadly interested in
      multilevel and longitudinal analyses, psychometrics, and causal inference. My work sits at
      the intersection of educational outcomes and quantitative methods, with a particular focus
      on Chilean higher education.
    

    
      Before graduate school, I studied Social Work at the Universidad del Bio-Bio, Chile, and
      worked as a research assistant at the Centro de Investigación Educativa CIDCIE at the
      same university.
    

    
      I am currently working on causal inference designs and meta-analysis in educational research.
    

    Research Interests
    Multilevel Models · Longitudinal Analysis · Psychometrics · Causal Inference · Chilean Higher Education

    News & Updates
    News coming soon

  

  
  
    
      
      Contact Information

title: Research
text:
My current research focuses on the discussion and application of quantitative methods in education. Primarily, I am working on multilevel analysis and causal inference.

Working Papers


Multilevel analysis of PAES 2025 results

Paper dedicated to the multilevel analysis of PAES 2025 test scores in reading proficiency and mathematics proficiency M1.
[Draft available upon request]


Exploring the Probabilities of Being Admitted to Selective Universities in Chile in 2022
Rodríguez, C., Padilla, B., Mojoyinola, M., & Romero, David.
This paper analyzes the admission results of Chilean students who participated in the selective higher education admission process in 2022 by means of a binary logistic regression model using academic and non-academic predictors. The results show that non-academic attributes were relevant predictors of the probability of admission into selective universities, especially the students’ family income quintile and school sector, which challenges the principle of fairness among students.

Under Review



Works in Progress


Precautions in the Use of Propensity Scores in Educational Research
Padilla, B., & Aloe, A.
This paper presents arguments and examples regarding the precautions that researchers interested in using propensity scores should consider in accordance with the presence of confounders, colliders, and mediators in their causal structural models.

Early Stage



Multilevel Analysis for Longitudinal Data
Padilla, B., & Hoffman, L.
Book chapter dedicated to multilevel modeling approaches for Intensive Longitudinal Data, focusing on core concepts and offering recommendations on current best practices.

Early Stage
